items.title: Cleanup and race prevention: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this React: Component-Based Browser Applications misconception? Cleanup runs only when the whole application closes, not before an effect reruns.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. React cleans up before a changed effect reruns and on unmount; write setup and cleanup as a reversible pair.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. React cleans up before a changed effect reruns and on unmount; write setup and cleanup as a reversible pair.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked React: Component-Based Browser Applications correction rather than the documented misconception.

items.title: Cleanup and race prevention: verified implementation facet
items.prompt: Undo subscriptions and ignore or abort obsolete asynchronous results across rerenders and unmounts. Build a bounded artifact demonstrating setup/cleanup symmetry for window subscription; the locked browser verifier checks observable structure, behavior, or computed presentation.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted authoring draft. Runtime evidence does not replace independent human standards, pedagogy, accessibility, answer-oracle, and formal-exam review.

feedback.correct: The locked authoring verifier observed the declared bounded facet in both visible and hidden cases.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic authoring check failed; inspect the exact artifact, interaction state, viewport, and observable result without assuming hidden-case details.

criteria.description: Passes the visible and hidden deterministic checks for the declared implementation facet in its pinned authoring environment.
